info.title: DevFlow Agent Protocol
info.description: Protocol for runtime app inspection, interaction, and debugging in DevFlow-enabled applications.

servers.description: Agent HTTP server running inside the target application

port.description: Port the agent is listening on

tags.name: ui
tags.description: UI tree inspection, element queries, screenshots

tags.name: ui-actions
tags.description: UI interaction (tap, fill, scroll, gestures, etc.)

tags.name: device
tags.description: Device info, sensors, jobs, permissions, geolocation

get.description: Returns the agent's identity, platform, version, and running state.
Returns the capabilities and extensions supported by this agent.
Returns the full visual tree of the application. Windows are root nodes. Use query parameters to limit depth, select a tree layer, or scope to a subtree.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: Maximum depth of the tree to return. Unlimited if omitted.

parameters.name: rootId
parameters.description: Element ID to scope the tree to a subtree.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Additional data to include with each element.

200.description: Array of root elements (windows) with their children.

get.description: Returns a single element by its globally unique identifier.
Searches the visual tree for elements matching the given locator strategy and value.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Additional data to include with each element.

get.description: Returns the elements at the given screen coordinates, ordered deepest first (innermost element first).

200.description: Elements at the given coordinates (deepest first).

get.description: Captures a screenshot of the entire screen or a specific element.

parameters.name: elementId
parameters.description: Element to screenshot. If omitted, captures the full screen.

parameters.name: maxWidth
parameters.description: Maximum width in pixels. The image is scaled down if wider.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale mode. `native` returns at device pixel density; `auto` returns a reasonable size for agent consumption.

post.description: Taps on an element (by ID) or at absolute coordinates.
Clears any existing text and fills the element with the given text.
Clears any text content from the specified element.
Gives keyboard focus to the specified element.
Scrolls an element by delta, to an item index, or to a named position.
Navigates the application to the specified route or page.
Resizes a window or element to the given dimensions.
Performs a back navigation gesture in the application.
Sends a key press event to the focused element or the application.
Executes a sequence of pointer actions (move, down, up, pause) to perform complex gestures like swipe, pinch, or drag.
Executes multiple actions in sequence. Optionally continues on error.

get.description: Returns the current value of a named property on an element.

put.description: Sets the value of a named property on an element and returns the new value.

200.description: The new property value after setting.

get.description: Returns all active WebView contexts in the application.

post.description: Evaluates a JavaScript expression inside a WebView context and returns the result.

get.description: Returns a simplified snapshot of the DOM tree in the WebView.

parameters.name: contextId
parameters.description: WebView context ID. Uses the default context if omitted.

post.description: Executes a CSS selector query against the WebView DOM and returns matching nodes.

get.description: Returns the HTML source of the WebView page.

parameters.name: contextId
parameters.description: WebView context ID. Uses the default context if omitted.

post.description: Navigates the WebView to the specified URL.
Clicks a DOM element matching the given CSS selector inside the WebView.
Clears and fills a DOM input element matching the given CSS selector.
Inserts text into the currently focused element in the WebView without clearing existing content.

get.description: Captures a screenshot of the WebView content.

parameters.name: contextId
parameters.description: WebView context ID. Uses the default context if omitted.

get.description: Returns which profiling features are supported on the current platform.

post.description: Starts a new profiling session with the given sample interval.

get.description: Returns profiling samples, markers, and spans collected since the given cursors. Use cursor values from the previous response to poll for new data.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return per category.

200.description: Profiler batch of samples, markers, and spans.

post.description: Publishes a timestamped marker event into the active profiling session.
Publishes a completed span into the active profiling session.

get.description: Returns aggregated performance hotspots from the profiling session.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of hotspots to return.

get.description: Returns captured HTTP requests, optionally filtered by host, method, or status code.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of requests to return.

delete.description: Clears all captured network requests from the buffer.

get.description: Returns full details for a captured network request, including headers and body content.
Returns application log entries, optionally filtered by source and level.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: Number of entries to skip (for pagination).

get.description: Returns hardware and OS information about the device.
Returns display dimensions, density, and orientation.
Returns battery level, state, and power source.
Returns network connectivity status and connection profiles.
Returns application name, version, build number, and package ID.
Returns the list of sensors and their availability/active status.

post.description: Starts collecting data from the named sensor. Readings are delivered via the WebSocket sensor channel.
Stops collecting data from the named sensor.

get.description: Returns platform background jobs known to the agent. Android reports WorkManager work IDs, tags, states, and run attempts. iOS and Mac Catalyst report pending BGTaskScheduler requests.

post.description: Attempts to trigger the named platform background job. iOS and Mac Catalyst submit a BGTaskRequest for the identifier; pass `type` when the task is known to be an app refresh task. Android currently reports `success=false` because WorkManager worker types and request inputs cannot be reconstructed safely from the listed work ID or tags.

parameters.name: identifier
parameters.description: Platform job identifier returned by the list endpoint.

get.description: Returns the status of all known permissions.
Returns the current status of a specific permission.
Returns the device's current geographic location.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the location request.

get.description: Returns all preference entries, optionally scoped to a shared container.

delete.description: Deletes all preferences, optionally scoped to a shared container.

get.description: Returns the value of a specific preference key.

put.description: Creates or updates a preference entry.

delete.description: Deletes a specific preference by key.
Deletes all values from secure storage.

get.description: Returns the value stored under the given key in secure storage.

put.description: Stores a value in secure storage under the given key.

delete.description: Deletes a specific value from secure storage.

UnsupportedCapability.description: Capability not supported on this platform.

agent-status.title: AgentStatus
agent-status.description: Agent status response providing runtime information about the agent, device, and application.

agent.description: Information about the DevFlow agent itself.

name.description: Name of the DevFlow agent implementation.

framework.description: The UI layer the agent is instrumented for (for example 'maui' or 'native').

platform.description: The target platform the agent is running on.

device.description: Information about the device or emulator/simulator.

running.description: Whether the application is currently running and responsive.

capabilities.description: Boolean status summary for agent capabilities.

jobs.description: Whether platform background jobs are supported by this agent.

uptime.description: Agent uptime as an ISO 8601 duration (e.g. 'PT1H30M15S').

type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

status.description: The HTTP status code for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

errorCode.description: Machine-readable error code identifying the category of failure.

agent-capabilities.title: AgentCapabilities
agent-capabilities.description: Capability discovery response describing what the agent supports.

agent.description: Information about the DevFlow agent itself.

name.description: Name of the DevFlow agent implementation.

framework.description: The UI framework the agent is instrumented for.

capabilities.description: Map of capability namespaces to their details. Keys are dot-separated namespaces.

features.description: List of supported features within this capability namespace.

additionalProperties.description: Capability details including version, features, and optional extra fields.

extensions.description: Map of extension namespaces (reverse-domain notation) to their route registrations.

ElementId.description: Globally unique element identifier within the visual tree.

CoordinateSystem.description: Coordinate system for bounding rectangles and points.

bounds-info.title: BoundsInfo
bounds-info.description: Bounding rectangle for a visual element.

x.description: The x-coordinate of the top-left corner.

y.description: The y-coordinate of the top-left corner.

width.description: The width of the bounding rectangle.

height.description: The height of the bounding rectangle.

coordinate.description: The coordinate system for this bounding rectangle.

element-info.title: ElementInfo
element-info.description: Core visual tree element model representing a single node in the UI hierarchy.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this element.

parentId.description: Identifier of the parent element, or null for root elements.

type.description: Short type name of the element (e.g. 'Button').

framework.description: The UI framework that owns this element.

automationId.description: Automation identifier set by the developer for testing purposes.

text.description: Visible text content of the element.

value.description: Current value for input elements (e.g. text field content, slider value).

role.description: Semantic role of the element for accessibility and interaction purposes.

traits.description: Semantic traits describing the element's capabilities and behavior.

state.description: Current interactive and visual state of the element.

displayed.description: Whether the element is currently visible on screen.

enabled.description: Whether the element is enabled for user interaction.

selected.description: Whether the element is currently selected.

focused.description: Whether the element currently has input focus.

opacity.description: Opacity of the element from fully transparent (0) to fully opaque (1).

gestures.description: Gesture types recognized by this element.

classes.description: CSS or style classes applied to this element.

nativeView.description: Information about the underlying native platform view.

properties.description: Key-value pairs of native view properties.

frameworkProperties.description: Framework-specific key-value pairs not captured by standard fields.

items.description: Child element in the visual tree.

children.description: Child elements forming the subtree under this element.

LocatorStrategy.description: Strategy used to locate an element in the visual tree.

IncludeOption.description: Optional data to include in the action response.

TapRequest.description: Request to perform a tap/click action on an element or at coordinates.

elementId.description: Target element identifier. Either elementId or coordinates must be provided.

x.description: X coordinate for the tap. Used when tapping by position rather than element.

y.description: Y coordinate for the tap. Used when tapping by position rather than element.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

ActionResponse.description: Response returned after executing an action, optionally including a screenshot and/or visual tree.

error.description: Error details if the action failed.

screenshot.description: Base64-encoded screenshot taken after the action, if requested via include.

tree.description: Visual tree snapshot taken after the action, if requested via include.

FillRequest.description: Request to fill text into an input element.

text.description: Text content to fill into the element.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

ClearRequest.description: Request to clear the content of an input element.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

FocusRequest.description: Request to move input focus to an element.

elementId.description: Target element identifier to receive focus.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

ScrollRequest.description: Request to perform a scroll action on a scrollable element.

elementId.description: Target scrollable element identifier. If null, scrolls the primary scrollable view.

deltaX.description: Horizontal scroll delta in device-independent pixels.

deltaY.description: Vertical scroll delta in device-independent pixels.

animated.description: Whether the scroll should be animated.

itemIndex.description: Index of the item to scroll to in a list or collection view.

groupIndex.description: Index of the group containing the target item.

scrollToPosition.description: Alignment of the target item within the viewport after scrolling.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

NavigateRequest.description: Request to navigate to a named route or URI within the application.

route.description: Route or URI to navigate to.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

ResizeRequest.description: Request to resize a window or element.

elementId.description: Target window element identifier. If null, resizes the main application window.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

BackRequest.description: Request to perform a back navigation action.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

KeyRequest.description: Request to send a key press event.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

GestureAction.description: A single action within a gesture sequence.

type.description: The type of gesture action to perform.

duration.description: Duration of this action in milliseconds.

button.description: Pointer button index (0 = primary, 1 = middle, 2 = secondary).

GestureRequest.description: Request to perform a complex gesture as a sequence of pointer actions.

actions.description: Ordered sequence of gesture actions to perform.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

BatchAction.description: A single action within a batch, combining the action type with its parameters.

action.description: The type of action to perform.

x.description: X coordinate (for tap and gesture actions).

y.description: Y coordinate (for tap and gesture actions).

animated.description: Whether scroll should be animated (for scroll action).

itemIndex.description: Item index to scroll to (for scroll action).

groupIndex.description: Group index for scroll target (for scroll action).

route.description: Route to navigate to (for navigate action).

BatchRequest.description: Request to perform multiple actions in sequence as a single batch operation.

actions.description: Ordered list of actions to perform.

include.description: Optional data to include in the response.

continueOnError.description: Whether to continue executing remaining actions if one fails.

WebViewContext.description: Represents a discovered WebView context within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for this WebView context.

elementId.description: Identifier linking this WebView to its corresponding element in the native UI tree.

url.description: Current URL loaded in the WebView.

title.description: Page title of the WebView content.

ready.description: Whether the WebView is ready for interaction (page loaded and DOM available).

EvaluateRequest.description: Request to evaluate a JavaScript expression in a WebView context.

contextId.description: Target WebView context identifier. If null, uses the default or only available context.

returnByValue.description: Whether to return the result by value (serialized) rather than by reference.

EvaluateResponse.description: Response from evaluating a JavaScript expression.

result.description: The result of the JavaScript evaluation as a JSON value.

exceptionDetails.description: Exception details if the evaluation threw an error.

lineNumber.description: Line number where the exception occurred.

columnNumber.description: Column number where the exception occurred.

DomQueryRequest.description: Request to query the DOM using a CSS selector.

selector.description: CSS selector to query the DOM.

WebViewNavigateRequest.description: Request to navigate a WebView to a URL.

url.description: URL to navigate the WebView to.

WebViewInputClickRequest.description: Request to click an element within a WebView using a CSS selector.

selector.description: CSS selector of the element to click.

WebViewInputFillRequest.description: Request to fill text into a WebView input element identified by a CSS selector.

selector.description: CSS selector of the input element to fill.

text.description: Text content to fill into the input element.

WebViewInputTextRequest.description: Request to type text into the currently focused element within a WebView.

text.description: Text to type into the focused element.

ProfilerCapabilities.description: Describes the profiler capabilities available on the current platform and agent.

managedMemorySupported.description: Whether managed heap memory measurement is supported.

nativeMemorySupported.description: Whether native memory measurement is supported.

gcSupported.description: Whether garbage collection counter reporting is supported.

cpuPercentSupported.description: Whether CPU percentage measurement is supported.

frameTimingsEstimated.description: Whether frame timings are estimated rather than measured directly.

nativeFrameTimingsSupported.description: Whether native frame timing measurement is supported.

jankEventsSupported.description: Whether jank frame detection events are supported.

uiThreadStallSupported.description: Whether UI thread stall detection is supported.

threadCountSupported.description: Whether thread count reporting is supported.

ProfilerSessionInfo.description: Information about an active or completed profiler session.

sessionId.description: Unique identifier for this profiler session.

startedAtUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the profiler session started.

sampleIntervalMs.description: Interval between profiler samples in milliseconds.

isActive.description: Whether the profiler session is currently active.

ProfilerSample.description: A single profiler sample capturing a point-in-time snapshot of performance metrics.

tsUtc.description: UTC timestamp when this sample was captured.

fps.description: Frames per second at the time of sampling.

worstFrameTimeMs.description: Worst (maximum) frame time in milliseconds during the sample interval.

managedBytes.description: Managed heap memory usage in bytes.

gc0.description: Number of generation 0 garbage collections since last sample.

gc1.description: Number of generation 1 garbage collections since last sample.

gc2.description: Number of generation 2 garbage collections since last sample.

cpuPercent.description: CPU usage percentage at the time of sampling.

threadCount.description: Total number of threads in the process.

jankFrameCount.description: Number of janky (dropped/slow) frames since last sample.

uiThreadStallCount.description: Number of UI thread stalls since last sample.

ProfilerMarker.description: A discrete profiler marker event representing a notable occurrence.

tsUtc.description: UTC timestamp when this marker was recorded.

name.description: Human-readable name of the marker event.

payloadJson.description: Optional JSON-encoded payload with additional marker data.

ProfilerSpan.description: A profiler span representing a timed operation, compatible with OpenTelemetry span semantics.

parentSpanId.description: Identifier of the parent span, or null for root spans.

startTsUtc.description: UTC timestamp when this span started.

endTsUtc.description: UTC timestamp when this span ended, or null if still in progress.

durationMs.description: Duration of the span in milliseconds.

threadId.description: Identifier of the thread this span executed on.

screen.description: Screen or page name where this span occurred.

elementPath.description: Path to the element in the visual tree associated with this span.

tagsJson.description: Optional JSON-encoded tags with additional span metadata.

error.description: Error message if the span ended with an error.

ProfilerBatch.description: A batch of profiler data including samples, markers, and spans with cursor positions for pagination.

sessionId.description: Identifier of the profiler session this batch belongs to.

samples.description: Array of profiler samples in this batch.

markers.description: Array of profiler markers in this batch.

spans.description: Array of profiler spans in this batch.

sampleCursor.description: Cursor position for the next page of samples.

markerCursor.description: Cursor position for the next page of markers.

spanCursor.description: Cursor position for the next page of spans.

isActive.description: Whether the profiler session is still actively collecting data.

ProfilerHotspot.description: A profiler hotspot identifying a frequently occurring or slow operation.

name.description: Name of the operation that is a hotspot.

screen.description: Screen or page where this hotspot occurs.

count.description: Number of times this operation was observed.

errorCount.description: Number of times this operation resulted in an error.

avgDurationMs.description: Average duration of this operation in milliseconds.

NetworkRequestSummary.description: Summary of a captured network request with key metadata and timing.

id.description: Unique identifier for this network request.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the request was initiated.

statusCode.description: HTTP status code of the response, or null if the request has not completed.

statusText.description: HTTP status text of the response (e.g. 'OK', 'Not Found').

error.description: Error message if the request failed.

requestSize.description: Size of the request body in bytes.

responseSize.description: Size of the response body in bytes.

NetworkRequestDetail.description: Detailed view of a captured network request including headers and body content.

requestBody.description: Request body content as a string.

responseBody.description: Response body content as a string.

requestBodyTruncated.description: Whether the request body was truncated due to size limits.

responseBodyTruncated.description: Whether the response body was truncated due to size limits.

LogEntry.description: A single log entry from the application.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the log entry was recorded.

level.description: Severity level of the log entry.

exception.description: Exception details if the log entry is associated with an error.

DeviceInfo.description: Hardware and platform information about the device.

DisplayInfo.description: Display characteristics of the device screen.

density.description: Display pixel density (ratio of physical to logical pixels).

refreshRate.description: Display refresh rate in Hz, or null if unavailable.

level.description: Battery charge level from empty (0) to full (1).

available.description: Whether this sensor is available on the device.

active.description: Whether this sensor is currently active and streaming data.

PlatformJob.description: A platform background job. Android jobs expose WorkManager fields; iOS and Mac Catalyst jobs expose BGTaskScheduler fields.

id.description: Android WorkManager UUID. Omitted for iOS and Mac Catalyst BGTaskScheduler requests.

identifier.description: Platform job identifier. For iOS and Mac Catalyst this is the BGTask identifier.

tags.description: Android WorkManager tags associated with the work.

state.description: Android WorkManager state, such as ENQUEUED, RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, BLOCKED, or CANCELLED.

type.description: iOS or Mac Catalyst BGTask request type.

earliestBeginDate.description: Earliest begin date reported by BGTaskScheduler, if present.

JobListResponse.description: Response returned when listing platform background jobs.

platform.description: Platform name reported by the agent.

type.description: Platform scheduler implementation, such as WorkManager or BGTaskScheduler.

supported.description: Whether the current platform supports listing jobs.

runSupported.description: Whether listed jobs can be triggered by this agent.

jobs.description: Background jobs reported by the platform.

error.description: Platform-specific error message if listing failed.

JobRunRequest.description: Optional request body used when triggering a platform background job.

type.description: iOS or Mac Catalyst BGTask request type. If omitted, the agent resolves it from pending requests when possible and otherwise defaults to processing.

JobRunResponse.description: Response returned after attempting to trigger a platform background job.

success.description: Whether the job trigger request was submitted successfully.

supported.description: Whether triggering this job is supported on the current platform.

type.description: BGTask request type used on iOS or Mac Catalyst.

altitude.description: Altitude in meters above sea level, or null if unavailable.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the location was determined.

PreferenceValueType.description: Data type of a preference value.

value.description: Preference value. The actual JSON type depends on the type field.

type.description: Data type of the preference value.

PreferenceSetRequest.description: Request to set a preference value.

value.description: The value to set for the preference.

type.description: Data type of the value. If null, the type is auto-detected from the JSON value.

sharedName.description: Shared preference container name. If null, uses the default container.

SecureStorageSetRequest.description: Request to set a value in secure (encrypted) storage.

value.description: The string value to store securely.
